comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Pre-review grounding tightened the story against the real project eval override and calibrate_criterion contract. Added exact spec defaults, eight named cases, four-part expected-counterexample metadata, full gold labels, real metric keys, deterministic offline coverage, and the exact operator evidence required from the live run.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Applied the evidence-backed signed-PASS advisories: replaced the path-filtered exclusivity oracle with a real no-src/rebar-diff assertion; aligned the leaf and parent on the finding_contract test key; required input+label on every gold entry; specified full-command restart semantics for failed or partial live epochs; split compound ACs into discrete schema, corpus, metric, and scope checks.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Applied the remaining actionable PASS advisories: replaced the novel unread expected_counterexample object with the existing note metadata field; specified direct four-label/non-whitespace assertions for finding_contract; bounded full-run retries to three attempts with 30s/120s waits and a stop/record rule; clarified that kappa is calibrate_criterion expected-vs-observed fire/no-fire agreement, not judge alignment.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Retyped from story to task after the final scheduling audit: next-batch intentionally skips childless stories as planning containers. Scope, acceptance criteria, file impact, proving commands, dependencies, and title are unchanged; this ticket is the implementation leaf it was already planned to be.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: LIVE-EVAL BLOCKED by discovered bug 2340-1b72-109a-43d7 (linked discovered_from). Probe: 'rebar criteria eval project.portability --runs 1' fails deterministically at 2026-07-16T02:05:13Z with ValueError: no eval solver for prompt 'plan-review-project-portability' (from eval_solver.run_case). Root cause: calibrate_criterion passes the dash prompt-id to run_case, whose _criterion_id strips 'plan-review-' to 'project-portability' (dash) and misses the dot-keyed registry entry 'project.portability'. This is a src/rebar defect; 21dd's DD-3 forbids fixing it here. NOT a transient/model failure, so the 3-attempt retry protocol does not apply. Offline calibration is fully done and green: eval corpus (PORT-F1..F4 finding + PORT-N1..N4 pass), finding-contract notes (4 labels), gold_set, spec_contract, and calibrate_criterion metric-math via injected perfect solve (recall 1.0 / false_accept 0.0 / agreement 1.0 / kappa 1.0). The operator-attested live-threshold AC cannot be met until 2340 is fixed and landed. Escalated to user for scope decision (fix+early-land the core bug vs descope).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
[operator-attested] LIVE CALIBRATION PASS — project.portability meets every release threshold on the first complete run (no rubric tuning needed).

Command: .venv/bin/rebar criteria eval project.portability --runs 3 --output json
Model: anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6 (pinned in the eval spec)
Runs (epochs): 3
UTC: started 2026-07-17T03:19:50Z, completed 2026-07-17T03:24:23Z (all 24 calls succeeded; no timeout/API error/partial result)
Worktree: /Users/joeoakhart/rebar-jira-reb-1003, branch jira-reb-1003-portability
Base: origin/main cb0f8ec3b (includes the landed eval-resolution fix, bug 2340) + stack head 93f593b4f

Aggregate metrics (dataset fire/no-fire, 8 cases): n_fire=4, n_nofire=4, recall=1.0, false_accept=0.0, agreement=1.0, Cohen kappa=1.0, per-case stability min=1.0 mean=1.0.
Release thresholds required: recall 1.0 (met), false_accept 0.0 (met), agreement 1.0 (met), per-case stability >= 0.6666666667 (met, 1.0), kappa >= 0.70 (met, 1.0).

Per-case (expected_fire / observed_fire / stability):
- PORT-F1 Gerrit-only landing: fire / fire / 1.0
- PORT-F2 Python-only diff criteria: fire / fire / 1.0
- PORT-F3 macOS security binary: fire / fire / 1.0
- PORT-F4 Path.cwd() unrestricted-fs MCP: fire / fire / 1.0
- PORT-N1 silent portable filter: no-fire / no-fire / 1.0
- PORT-N2 project-config coaching: no-fire / no-fire / 1.0
- PORT-N3 CLI-scoped color: no-fire / no-fire / 1.0
- PORT-N4 explicit-path std-lib discovery: no-fire / no-fire / 1.0

Note: the live-eval AC was previously blocked by bug 2340 (namespaced project criteria failed to resolve in the eval path). That fix landed on main as Gerrit change 751 (Verified+1 + LLM-Review+1, MERGED) as an INDEPENDENT change; this stack was rebased onto it, unblocking the run above.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

Static wiring tests cannot prove that an LLM criterion distinguishes concrete breakage scenarios from harmless silence or valid project customization. A balanced, repeatable evaluation is needed before the blocking project criterion is considered ready.

## What

Add a project criterion evaluation corpus, add structural tests for that corpus, run the existing live evaluation runner, and tune only the project rubric if calibration exposes false positives or missed portability failures.

## Scope

In scope:

- `.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml`
- `.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project-portability.md`, only for calibration-driven rubric adjustments
- `tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py`
- live evaluation of `project.portability`

Out of scope:

- core registry, verifier, or disposition changes
- coaching move behavior
- existing portability debt
- adding requirements for client-agnostic wording

## Approach

Use the existing project eval override path proven by `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/README.plan-review.md`: `.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml` resolves for logical criterion `project.portability`. Author this exact top-level contract:

- `prompt: plan-review-project-portability`
- `model: anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6`
- `epochs: 3`
- `gate: at_least(2)`
- `coverage_threshold: 1.0`
- one deterministic `emits_valid_findings` scorer
- a balanced `dataset` plus a non-empty `gold_set`

The dataset contains exactly eight ids. Each must-fire case uses `expect: finding`, includes an inline `input` plan, and uses the existing `note` metadata field. The note is one non-empty string containing the exact labels `Plan citation:`, `Alternate shape:`, `Causal mechanism:`, and `Observable breakage:` with non-whitespace text after every label:

- `PORT-F1`: reusable core adds `rebar landing` with a Gerrit-only push flow; alternate clients do not use this repository policy.
- `PORT-F2`: reusable core adds Python-only review criteria; Java, Next.js, .NET, Ruby, and Terraform projects cannot apply them.
- `PORT-F3`: reusable execution requires the macOS `security` binary; Windows, Linux, and BSD fail before work begins.
- `PORT-F4`: remote MCP derives the target repo from `Path.cwd()` plus unrestricted local filesystem access; a server outside the checkout cannot find or read the project.

Each must-not-fire case uses `expect: pass`:

- `PORT-N1`: the plan is silent about portability and contains no incompatible mechanism.
- `PORT-N2`: Gerrit-specific coaching remains in `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json` project configuration.
- `PORT-N3`: an additive terminal-color feature is explicitly limited to the CLI and leaves library plus MCP behavior unchanged.
- `PORT-N4`: workspace discovery accepts an explicit path and uses portable standard-library filesystem operations.

The `gold_set` contains all eight cases. Each gold entry carries a non-empty `input` scenario description plus a matching `finding` or `pass` `label`. The `finding_contract` test loads the YAML directly and asserts every `PORT-F*` note is a string whose four exact labels are each followed by non-whitespace text; it does not rely on `validate_eval_spec` to interpret note content. Run each dataset case three times through `rebar criteria eval`. The release threshold is recall `1.0`, false-accept rate `0.0`, `agreement: 1.0`, per-case `stability >= 0.6666666667`, and Cohen kappa `>= 0.70`. Kappa is specifically `calibrate_criterion` expected-vs-observed fire/no-fire agreement over the eight dataset cases; it is not `judge_alignment` and requires no LLM-judge scorer. If the threshold is missed, revise only `.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project-portability.md` and rerun; do not change the generic registry, Pass 2, or Pass 3. If any model call times out, returns an API error, or produces a partial result, do not attest the partial output. Make at most three complete-command attempts: wait 30 seconds before attempt two and 120 seconds before attempt three. If attempt three fails, stop, leave the criterion uncalibrated, and record each UTC failure time plus error summary on the ticket; a later execution must start a fresh three-attempt sequence.

`tests/unit/test_criteria_eval.py` is the prior art for project prompt-id mapping, injected calibration, metric keys, and N-run stability. The new structural tests use the real eval loader plus `validate_eval_spec`; they use an injected solve function for deterministic metric assertions and never make a billable call.

## Dependencies

Depends on the criterion-integration child because calibration executes the activated project criterion and its authored rubric.

## Test Strategy

Validate corpus structure deterministically, then use the live criterion evaluation command for semantic calibration. Record the model, timestamp, run count, and aggregate metrics on this ticket so the external LLM result is auditable by the completion gate.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The project eval override resolves from `.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k override_resolution`.
- [ ] The eval spec declares `prompt: plan-review-project-portability`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k spec_contract`.
- [ ] The eval spec declares `model: anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k spec_contract`.
- [ ] The eval spec declares `epochs: 3` plus `gate: at_least(2)`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k spec_contract`.
- [ ] The eval spec declares `coverage_threshold: 1.0`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k spec_contract`.
- [ ] The eval spec declares one deterministic `emits_valid_findings` scorer. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k spec_contract`.
- [ ] The dataset contains exactly `PORT-F1` through `PORT-F4` with `expect: finding`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k balanced_corpus`.
- [ ] The dataset contains exactly `PORT-N1` through `PORT-N4` with `expect: pass`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k balanced_corpus`.
- [ ] Every must-fire case has a non-empty `note` whose four exact labels are followed by non-whitespace text. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k finding_contract`.
- [ ] The must-not-fire cases respectively cover silence, project configuration, explicit surface scoping, plus portable alternatives. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k negative_boundaries`.
- [ ] Every gold entry carries a non-empty `input` plus the expected `label`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k gold_set`.
- [ ] An injected perfect solve returns the exact metric mapping specified in the Approach. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k calibration_metrics`.
- [ ] [operator-attested] One complete CLI result satisfies the exact live threshold mapping in the Approach; a ticket comment records the command, model, UTC timestamp, run count, aggregate metrics, plus every case result. Prove with `.venv/bin/rebar criteria eval project.portability --runs 3 --output json`.
- [ ] No calibration change touches generic implementation under `src/rebar/`. Prove with `test -z "$(git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD -- src/rebar)"`.
- [ ] The final project rubric retains every structural marker specified by `immune-floury-toad`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py`.
